name: Stuffed Mushrooms III
recipeInstructions:
1. Wipe mushrooms with a damp cloth. Carefully remove stems and set aside.
2. In a small, heat-resistant, non-metallic bowl or cup, melt 3 tablespoons butter or margarine in the Microwave Oven for 30 seconds.
3. Brush mushroom caps with melted butter or margarine and place, hollow-side-up, in a shallow, heat-resistant, non-metallic baking dish. Sprinkle mushroom caps with salt and pepper to taste.
4. Finely chop reserved mushroom stems.
5. In a medium-sized, heat-resistant, non-metallic bowl, melt 1/4 cup butter in the Microwave Oven for 30 seconds. Add onions and chopped mushroom stems; stir to combine.
6. Heat, uncovered, in the Microwave Oven for 3 minutes or until onions and mushrooms are tender. Blend in flour until smooth.
7. Gradually stir in heavy cream until smooth. Heat, uncovered, in the Microwave Oven for 2 1/2 minutes or until thickened and smooth. Stir in chopped parsley.
8. Fill each mushroom cap with some of the mushroom mixture.
9. If desired, sprinkle a little grated Swiss cheese over each stuffed mushroom (refrigerate if not going to serve them at this time).
10. Heat, uncovered, in a heat-resistant, non-metallic pie pan in the Microwave Oven for 3 minutes just before serving. (If refrigerated, heating will take an additional 1 minute.)
